course_outcomes: Explain why customs valuation rules are needed
Describe the legal and institutional framework of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement
Apply the six valuation methods in the correct order
Explain transaction value, Article 8 adjustments, related parties, and reasonable doubts
Identify prohibited valuation methods and key procedural safeguards

units.title: Why Customs Valuation Matters
units.unit_goal: Understand why valuation rules exist and why customs value matters in international trade.

lessons.title: Why Valuation Rules Exist
lessons.goal: Understand the purpose of customs valuation rules.
micro_steps: Explain why customs valuation rules are needed
Explain what arbitrary or fictitious values are
Explain why the system must be fair, uniform, and neutral

lessons.title: Why Customs Value Matters
lessons.goal: Understand why customs value is important in ad valorem duty systems.
micro_steps: Explain what happens when duty is ad valorem
Explain why customs value is the duty base
Explain how inflated values can undermine tariff concessions

lessons.title: Trade Impact Match
lessons.goal: Match valuation problems to their trade consequences.
micro_steps: Match inflated value to unfairly high duty
Match fair valuation to neutral treatment
Match arbitrary value to trade distortion

lessons.title: Foundation Quiz
lessons.goal: Check your understanding of the need for customs valuation rules.
micro_steps: Identify the purpose of valuation rules
Recognize the effect of inflated customs values
Apply the idea of ad valorem valuation

lessons.title: Unit 1 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show that you understand why customs valuation exists and why it matters.
micro_steps: Explain the purpose of customs valuation rules
Explain why ad valorem duties depend on customs value
Explain why exporters care about unfair valuation

units.title: GATT Background and Legal Context
units.unit_goal: Understand the GATT framework and how customs valuation rules developed historically.

lessons.title: GATT and Customs Valuation
lessons.goal: Understand the place of Article VII within the GATT framework.
micro_steps: Identify Article VII as the main valuation article
Recognize related GATT articles
Explain the broader trade facilitation purpose of customs rules

lessons.title: Article Map
lessons.goal: Recall the related GATT provisions around valuation.
micro_steps: Match Article V to freedom of transit
Match Article VIII to fees and formalities
Match Article IX to marks of origin
Match Article X to publication and administration

lessons.title: From GATT 1947 to WTO Rules
lessons.goal: Understand historical development from GATT 1947 through the Tokyo and Uruguay Rounds.
micro_steps: Explain what GATT 1947 prohibited
Explain why the Tokyo Round addressed valuation
Explain why the Uruguay Round made valuation rules binding on WTO Members

lessons.title: History Match
lessons.goal: Place customs valuation milestones in their proper sequence.
micro_steps: Match GATT 1947 to 'actual value' language
Match Tokyo Round to 1979 protocol
Match Uruguay Round to 1994 WTO Agreement

lessons.title: Unit 2 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate understanding of the legal origin of the valuation rules.
micro_steps: Explain Article VII
Explain why the original rules were considered inadequate
Explain how the WTO Agreement emerged

units.title: Structure of the Agreement
units.unit_goal: Understand the layout of the Customs Valuation Agreement, including parts, annexes, and committees.

lessons.title: Parts of the Agreement
lessons.goal: Understand the main parts of the Agreement.
micro_steps: Identify what Part I covers
Identify what Part II covers
Identify what Part III covers
Identify what Part IV covers

lessons.title: Article Map Inside Part I
lessons.goal: Understand the main article groupings within Part I.
micro_steps: Identify where the six methods are found
Identify the article on currency conversion
Identify the articles on confidentiality, appeal, publication, release of goods, definitions, and written explanation

lessons.title: Annexes and Committees
lessons.goal: Understand the annexes and administrative bodies established under the Agreement.
micro_steps: Identify what each annex contains
Explain why annexes are legally important
Differentiate the WTO Committee from the WCO Technical Committee

lessons.title: Structure Match
lessons.goal: Match articles, annexes, and committees to their functions.
micro_steps: Match Article 11 to appeal
Match Annex I to interpretative notes
Match the WTO Committee to policy issues
Match the Technical Committee to technical interpretation

lessons.title: Unit 3 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show that you understand the architecture of the Agreement.
micro_steps: List the main parts
Identify the function of key articles
Explain the role of annexes and committees

units.title: The Six Valuation Methods
units.unit_goal: Memorize and understand the six valuation methods and the required order of use.

lessons.title: Method 1 and Its Primacy
lessons.goal: Understand why transaction value comes first.
micro_steps: Identify transaction value as Method 1
Explain why it has primacy
Connect primacy to fairness and commercial reality

lessons.title: Methods 2 to 6
lessons.goal: Learn the remaining five valuation methods.
micro_steps: Identify Method 2
Identify Method 3
Identify Method 4
Identify Method 5
Identify Method 6

lessons.title: Order Matters
lessons.goal: Understand the mandatory hierarchy of valuation methods.
micro_steps: State the correct order of methods
Explain why customs must move method by method
Recognize that prohibited shortcuts are not allowed

lessons.title: Method Order Match
lessons.goal: Place all six methods in the correct order.
micro_steps: Arrange methods from 1 to 6
Differentiate identical from similar goods
Differentiate deductive from computed value

lessons.title: Unit 4 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate mastery of the six valuation methods and their sequence.
micro_steps: List all six methods
Explain which comes first and why
Explain that customs must respect the hierarchy

units.title: Method 1: Transaction Value
units.unit_goal: Understand transaction value deeply as the primary customs valuation method.

lessons.title: Basic Rule of Article 1.1
lessons.goal: Understand the core definition of transaction value.
micro_steps: Explain the basic rule in Article 1.1
Break down the four core elements
Recognize that transaction value is tied to an export sale

lessons.title: Price Actually Paid or Payable
lessons.goal: Understand what counts as the actual price.
micro_steps: Explain what 'price actually paid or payable' means
Recognize direct and indirect payment
Recognize payments made for the seller’s benefit

lessons.title: Discounts and Lower Prices
lessons.goal: Understand why lower price alone is not a reason to reject transaction value.
micro_steps: Explain why a low declared price is not automatically invalid
Explain why market prices do not automatically override transaction value
Recognize valid discounts

lessons.title: Sale for Export
lessons.goal: Understand the meaning of 'sold for export to the country of importation'.
micro_steps: Explain the need for a sale for export
Explain that seller location can vary
Explain that the sale must involve international movement of goods

lessons.title: Transaction Value Quiz
lessons.goal: Test understanding of the primary valuation method.
micro_steps: Identify the elements of transaction value
Distinguish valid lower prices from invalid reasons for rejection
Recognize a sale for export

lessons.title: Unit 5 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show strong understanding of transaction value.
micro_steps: Define transaction value
Explain price actually paid or payable
Explain sale for export and why Method 1 has primacy

units.title: Article 8 Adjustments
units.unit_goal: Understand what may be added to the price under Article 8 and the limits on those additions.

lessons.title: When Additions Are Allowed
lessons.goal: Understand the basic rule for Article 8 additions.
micro_steps: Explain that additions apply only if not already included
Explain the need for objective and quantifiable data
Explain that only listed additions may be used

lessons.title: Common Article 8 Additions
lessons.goal: Recognize the major types of permitted additions.
micro_steps: List common Article 8 additions
Explain what assists are in general terms
Explain why transport costs may be added

lessons.title: Items Commonly Excluded
lessons.goal: Recognize items that may be excluded in appropriate cases.
micro_steps: Identify interest payments as possible exclusions
Identify dividends as possible exclusions
Recognize post-importation services and marketing costs as possible exclusions

lessons.title: Adjustment Match
lessons.goal: Sort valuation items into additions or exclusions.
micro_steps: Match royalties to possible additions
Match containers to possible additions
Match post-importation services to possible exclusions
Match interest to possible exclusions

lessons.title: Unit 6 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate control over Article 8 additions and exclusions.
micro_steps: Explain when additions are allowed
List major Article 8 additions
Explain why customs cannot add unlisted items

units.title: Related Parties and Circumstances of Sale
units.unit_goal: Understand related-party valuation, what customs must examine, and how relationship affects the price test.

lessons.title: When Parties Are Related
lessons.goal: Understand the legal categories of related parties under Article 15.
micro_steps: List the types of related parties
Understand control-based relationships
Recall the 5 percent voting stock rule

lessons.title: Relationship Alone Is Not Enough
lessons.goal: Understand the core rule for related-party transactions.
micro_steps: Explain that mere relationship is not enough to reject transaction value
Explain the real question customs must ask
Connect the rule to fairness and commercial reality

lessons.title: Circumstances of Sale and Test Values
lessons.goal: Understand the two broad ways customs may examine related-party sales.
micro_steps: Explain review of circumstances surrounding the sale
Explain review using test values
Identify ways of proving the relationship did not influence price

lessons.title: Related Party Match
lessons.goal: Match relationship categories and proof concepts correctly.
micro_steps: Match family to related parties
Match common control to relatedness
Match normal industry practice to evidence of non-influence
Match cost plus average profit to evidence of non-influence

lessons.title: Unit 7 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand related-party valuation and the price influence test.
micro_steps: Define related parties
Explain the key test for related-party transactions
Explain circumstances of sale and test values

units.title: When Method 1 Cannot Be Used
units.unit_goal: Understand the main reasons transaction value may be rejected and how reasonable doubts operate.

lessons.title: Basic Reasons Method 1 May Fail
lessons.goal: Understand the main barriers to using transaction value.
micro_steps: Recognize there must be a sale
Recognize that some restrictions can disqualify Method 1
Recognize that undeterminable conditions can prevent Method 1
Recognize issues involving resale proceeds and related parties

lessons.title: Reasonable Doubts Procedure
lessons.goal: Understand what customs may do if it doubts the declared value.
micro_steps: Explain what customs may request
Explain what customs must tell the importer
Explain the right to respond
Explain why a final rejection must be in writing

lessons.title: Lower Price Is Not Enough
lessons.goal: Understand why low price alone does not justify rejection.
micro_steps: Explain why low market price alone is not enough
Explain why lower identical-goods price alone is not enough
Explain why discounts do not automatically invalidate Method 1

lessons.title: Procedure Match
lessons.goal: Put the reasonable doubts process in the correct order.
micro_steps: Match doubt to request for more information
Match request to importer explanation opportunity
Match final rejection to written notice

lessons.title: Unit 8 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate understanding of rejection of Method 1 and the reasonable doubts process.
micro_steps: List reasons Method 1 may fail
Explain why low price alone is not enough
Explain the reasonable doubts procedure

units.title: Prohibited Methods and the Fall-back Method
units.unit_goal: Understand prohibited valuation methods and how the fall-back method differs from arbitrary valuation.

lessons.title: Prohibited Methods
lessons.goal: Recognize the valuation methods that customs may not use.
micro_steps: List the prohibited methods
Explain why minimum values are prohibited
Explain why arbitrary and fictitious values are prohibited

lessons.title: Fall-back Method Basics
lessons.goal: Understand Method 6 in contrast to prohibited approaches.
micro_steps: Identify Method 6 as the last method
Explain why it is not the same as arbitrary valuation
Explain that even the fall-back method must stay within WTO rules

lessons.title: Allowed or Prohibited?
lessons.goal: Sort valuation approaches into allowed and prohibited groups.
micro_steps: Match minimum values to prohibited
Match price to third-country export to prohibited
Match fall-back method to allowed last resort
Match domestic selling price to prohibited

lessons.title: Unit 9 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you can identify what customs may never do in valuation.
micro_steps: List major prohibited methods
Explain why they are banned
Explain the fall-back method as the final lawful method

units.title: Importer Protections and Administration
units.unit_goal: Understand the administrative and procedural safeguards in the Agreement.

lessons.title: Confidentiality, Publication, Appeal
lessons.goal: Understand key importer protections and administrative obligations.
micro_steps: Identify the article on confidentiality
Identify the article on publication
Identify the importer’s right of appeal

lessons.title: Release of Goods and Written Explanation
lessons.goal: Understand procedural fairness when valuation is in dispute.
micro_steps: Identify the article on release of goods
Identify the importer’s right to a written explanation
Recognize the role of definitions in Article 15

lessons.title: Procedural Safeguards Match
lessons.goal: Match importer protections to the correct article or concept.
micro_steps: Match confidentiality to Article 10
Match appeal to Article 11
Match written explanation to Article 16
Match release of goods to Article 13

lessons.title: Unit 10 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate understanding of importer rights and administrative safeguards.
micro_steps: List major procedural safeguards
Explain the right of appeal
Explain why written explanation and release procedures matter

units.title: Accession, National Laws, and Notifications
units.unit_goal: Understand how WTO Members implement the Agreement through accession commitments, national legislation, and notification duties.

lessons.title: Accession Commitments
lessons.goal: Understand what WTO acceding Members must do in customs valuation.
micro_steps: Explain the baseline accession commitment
Explain why minimum values are unacceptable
Explain why reference prices and fixed schedules are problematic

lessons.title: National Legislation and Article 22
lessons.goal: Understand the relationship between WTO obligations and national law.
micro_steps: Identify Article 22
Explain why national legislation matters
Connect domestic law to WTO implementation

lessons.title: Notifications to the Committee
lessons.goal: Understand why members notify legislation and implementation decisions.
micro_steps: Explain why valuation legislation is notified
Explain the purpose of the Checklist of Issues
Explain the notification duties for the decisions on interest charges and carrier media

lessons.title: Implementation Match
lessons.goal: Match implementation obligations to their purpose.
micro_steps: Match accession to full implementation
Match legislation notification to transparency
Match Checklist of Issues to Committee review
Match decision notifications to implementation status

lessons.title: Unit 11 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand implementation, accession, and notification obligations.
micro_steps: Explain accession commitments
Explain the role of national legislation
Explain why notifications matter

units.title: Grand Mastery Path
units.unit_goal: Bring the whole Agreement together from purpose and structure to methods, restrictions, rights, and implementation.

lessons.title: Method Logic Quiz
lessons.goal: Review the six methods, their order, and the primacy of transaction value.
micro_steps: Identify all six methods
Apply the hierarchy correctly
Recognize when Method 1 is unavailable

lessons.title: Rights and Restrictions Quiz
lessons.goal: Review prohibited methods, reasonable doubts, appeal rights, and importer protections.
micro_steps: Identify prohibited methods
Apply the reasonable doubts process
Recall importer rights and procedural protections

lessons.title: Integrated Valuation Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Apply the Agreement to realistic customs valuation cases.
micro_steps: Assess whether Method 1 can be used
Identify possible Article 8 adjustments
Check for related-party concerns
Choose the next method if Method 1 fails
Spot prohibited customs approaches

lessons.title: Grand Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate full mastery of the WTO Agreement on Customs Valuation.
micro_steps: Explain why the Agreement exists
Explain its structure
Explain all six methods and their order
Explain transaction value, Article 8, related parties, and reasonable doubts
Explain importer protections and implementation obligations

lessons.title: Legendary Review Path
lessons.goal: Final mixed review for long-term retention and exam readiness.
micro_steps: Mixed GATT and WTO structure questions
Mixed valuation method questions
Mixed related-party and Article 8 questions
Mixed rights, restrictions, and implementation questions

daily_practice.description: Recycles weak lessons, commonly confused valuation methods, related-party concepts, and prohibited methods.
